generatedSentence: Anchored to his workstation in the opulent hotel, the installer, a man often afraid with latent arachnophobia, typed on his laptop, decoding ancient glyphs that seemed to emit a strange stimulant from the monitor, their glow reflecting in his teeth; despite his impressive achievements, he often felt underappreciated, constantly being blamed for system failures, a fallacy he maintains is mere political flack from rivals who monitor his emails held in digital escrow, though he occasionally finds small, heavenly victories, feeling an impish delight when a particularly stubborn heating issue finally resolves, making him open to the night's possibilities, ducking away from the monumental server racks. But this night brought an unforgettable twist, as a notorious villain, using a custom-built dumpy kart equipped with a tachyon emitter, caused widespread destruction across the nearby township, creating digital platoons of viruses that disrupted vital education networks, making the internet a perilous place for any mortal user; his rhythm of chaos was unmistakable, a forked path of data corruption that left system administrators in a waffle of despair, as he laughed, a sound like birds that babbled nonsense, declaring this his ultimate jackpot, leaving a trail of digital nectar luring unsuspecting intruders to isolated islands of data, each breach a calculated injury, and with a triumphant cry of "gotcha!" he disappeared, leaving the digital landscape in disarray, prompting a keen awareness of impending doom. The viceroy of the digital domain, a formidable doppelganger of the installer, emerged through a shimmering portal, his intentions as murky as a scraggy digital swamp, intent on payback for perceived slights, making the true installer feel unworthy of the impending confrontation; the digital realm, usually held by rigid objectivity, was now a chaotic blend of memes and warring factions, threatening to impeach the very fabric of reality, creating a massive data hurricane that threatened to bushwhack any unsuspecting user, inching closer to a homewrecker scenario for the entire network; the installer, feeling the digital nightfall approaching and a strange sense of gratitude for his own neutral stance, realized he had to go through this confrontation, facing off against this schmuck who consumed digital omelet as he mocked, his presence a dark, alluring nymphet of pure data, ready to unleash more viruses upstream. In the aftermath, the digital vehicle of the doppelganger lay broken on the virtual wharf, the villain having vanished with a defiant "teehee!", leaving behind a colossal wrinkle in the fabric of the network, which now needed a massive taxpayer-funded overhaul of bills and repairs; the initial galumphing efforts to restore order were slow, like a snail inching along a brook, as the burly network engineers, fuelled by cheap ale, tried to whack away the remaining digital detritus from the boulevard of servers; the gross amount of greywackes — digital detritus — had to be dumping into secure archives, as the very essence of the network, usually so normal and predictable, had been forever altered, its corrupted data now embalms like ancient relics, making the habitual use of digital eyewash a necessity; a solemn newscast reported on the strange event, suggesting it was the work of rogue djinns from a parallel dimension, the pilot in the cockpit of the recovery effort sighing at the monumental task ahead.
panelSentences: Anchored to his workstation in the opulent hotel, the installer, a man often afraid with latent arachnophobia, typed on his laptop, decoding ancient glyphs that seemed to emit a strange stimulant from the monitor, their glow reflecting in his teeth; despite his impressive achievements, he often felt underappreciated, constantly being blamed for system failures, a fallacy he maintains is mere political flack from rivals who monitor his emails held in digital escrow, though he occasionally finds small, heavenly victories, feeling an impish delight when a particularly stubborn heating issue finally resolves, making him open to the night's possibilities, ducking away from the monumental server racks.

But this night brought an unforgettable twist, as a notorious villain, using a custom-built dumpy kart equipped with a tachyon emitter, caused widespread destruction across the nearby township, creating digital platoons of viruses that disrupted vital education networks, making the internet a perilous place for any mortal user; his rhythm of chaos was unmistakable, a forked path of data corruption that left system administrators in a waffle of despair, as he laughed, a sound like birds that babbled nonsense, declaring this his ultimate jackpot, leaving a trail of digital nectar luring unsuspecting intruders to isolated islands of data, each breach a calculated injury, and with a triumphant cry of "gotcha!" he disappeared, leaving the digital landscape in disarray, prompting a keen awareness of impending doom.

The viceroy of the digital domain, a formidable doppelganger of the installer, emerged through a shimmering portal, his intentions as murky as a scraggy digital swamp, intent on payback for perceived slights, making the true installer feel unworthy of the impending confrontation; the digital realm, usually held by rigid objectivity, was now a chaotic blend of memes and warring factions, threatening to impeach the very fabric of reality, creating a massive data hurricane that threatened to bushwhack any unsuspecting user, inching closer to a homewrecker scenario for the entire network; the installer, feeling the digital nightfall approaching and a strange sense of gratitude for his own neutral stance, realized he had to go through this confrontation, facing off against this schmuck who consumed digital omelet as he mocked, his presence a dark, alluring nymphet of pure data, ready to unleash more viruses upstream.

In the aftermath, the digital vehicle of the doppelganger lay broken on the virtual wharf, the villain having vanished with a defiant "teehee!", leaving behind a colossal wrinkle in the fabric of the network, which now needed a massive taxpayer-funded overhaul of bills and repairs; the initial galumphing efforts to restore order were slow, like a snail inching along a brook, as the burly network engineers, fuelled by cheap ale, tried to whack away the remaining digital detritus from the boulevard of servers; the gross amount of greywackes — digital detritus — had to be dumping into secure archives, as the very essence of the network, usually so normal and predictable, had been forever altered, its corrupted data now embalms like ancient relics, making the habitual use of digital eyewash a necessity; a solemn newscast reported on the strange event, suggesting it was the work of rogue djinns from a parallel dimension, the pilot in the cockpit of the recovery effort sighing at the monumental task ahead.
rawPanelSentences: Anchored to his workstation in the opulent **hotel**, the **installer**, a man often **afraid** with latent **arachnophobia**, typed on his **laptop**, decoding ancient **glyphs** that seemed to emit a strange **stimulant** from the monitor, their glow reflecting in his **teeth**; despite his impressive **achievements**, he often felt **under**appreciated, constantly being **blamed** for system failures, a **fallacy** he **maintains** is mere political **flack** from rivals who monitor his **emails** held in digital **escrow**, though he occasionally finds small, **heavenly** victories, feeling an **impish** delight when a particularly stubborn **heating** issue finally resolves, making him **open** to the night's possibilities, **ducking** away from the **monumental** server racks.

But this night brought an **unforgettable** twist, as a notorious **villain**, using a custom-built **dumpy kart** equipped with a **tachyon** emitter, caused widespread **destruction** across the nearby **township**, creating digital **platoons** of **viruses** that disrupted vital **education** networks, making the **internet** a perilous place for any **mortal** user; his **rhythm** of chaos was unmistakable, a **forked** path of data corruption that left system administrators in a **waffle** of despair, as he laughed, a sound like **birds** that **babbled** nonsense, declaring this his ultimate **jackpot**, leaving a trail of digital **nectar** luring unsuspecting **intruders** to isolated **islands** of data, each breach a calculated **injury**, and with a triumphant cry of "**gotcha**!" he disappeared, leaving the digital landscape in disarray, prompting a **keen** awareness of impending doom.

The **viceroy** of the digital domain, a formidable **doppelganger** of the installer, emerged through a shimmering **portal**, his intentions as murky as a **scraggy** digital swamp, intent on **payback** for perceived slights, making the true installer feel **unworthy** of the impending confrontation; the digital realm, usually held by rigid **objectivity**, was now a chaotic blend of **memes** and warring **factions**, threatening to **impeach** the very fabric of reality, creating a massive data **hurricane** that threatened to **bushwhack** any unsuspecting user, inching closer to a **homewrecker** scenario for the entire network; the installer, feeling the digital **nightfall** approaching and a strange sense of **gratitude** for his own **neutral** stance, realized he had to **go** through this confrontation, facing off against this **schmuck** who consumed digital **omelet** as he mocked, his presence a dark, alluring **nymphet** of pure data, ready to unleash more **viruses** upstream.

In the aftermath, the digital **vehicle** of the doppelganger lay broken on the virtual **wharf**, the villain having vanished with a defiant "**teehee**!", leaving behind a colossal **wrinkle** in the fabric of the network, which now needed a massive **taxpayer**-funded overhaul of **bills** and repairs; the initial **galumphing** efforts to restore order were slow, like a **snail** inching along a **brook**, as the burly network engineers, fuelled by cheap **ale**, tried to **whack** away the remaining digital detritus from the **boulevard** of servers; the gross amount of **greywackes** — digital detritus — had to be **dumping** into secure archives, as the very essence of the network, usually so **normal** and predictable, had been forever altered, its corrupted data now **embalms** like ancient relics, making the habitual use of digital **eyewash** a necessity; a solemn **newscast** reported on the strange event, suggesting it was the work of rogue **djinns** from a **parallel** dimension, the pilot in the **cockpit** of the recovery effort sighing at the monumental task ahead.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, thick white gutters, clean black borders, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. A lanky installer wearing a glowing tool belt and a chipped augmented reality monocle. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The installer, wide-eyed and slightly hunched, his hands poised over a glowing laptop, surrounded by cryptic glyphs and exposed heating pipes in a dimly lit, futuristic server room. (Over-the-shoulder shot, tight focus on laptop screen). TOP-RIGHT PANEL: An impish villain with a chaotic grin, piloting a small, dumpy, high-tech kart through a digitally ravaged, neon-lit township, leaving a trail of shattered data streams. (Dynamic low-angle shot, looking up at the villain). BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The installer's doppelganger, a glitching, ethereal figure made of digital code, emerging from a shimmering, polygonal portal, his form radiating corrupted memes and viral energy, confronting the fearful installer. (Mid-shot, intense eye-level confrontation). BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: A panoramic view of a massive, burly digital wharf at dawn, where a wrecked, smoking vehicle resembling the villain's kart lies amidst debris, while a holographic newscast flickers in the background, showing a snail-like progress bar of network recovery. (Wide shot, high-angle, showing vastness of the digital landscape).

definitions.definition: A large town house or mansion; a grand private residence, especially in France.
An establishment that provides accommodation and other services for paying guests; normally larger than a guesthouse, and often one of a chain.
A restaurant; any dining establishment.
The letter H in the ICAO spelling alphabet.
The larger red property in the game of Monopoly, in contradistinction to houses.
The guest accommodation and dining section of a cruise ship.
Any of the poisonous umbelliferous plants, of the genera
The poison obtained from these Conium and Cicuta plants.
Any of several coniferous trees, of the genus Tsuga, that grow in North America; the wood of such trees.
To cause an increase in temperature of (an object or space); to cause to become hot (often with "up").
definitions.example: I'll heat up the water.

definitions.definition: To become hotter.
definitions.example: There's a pot of soup heating on the stove.

definitions.definition: To excite or make hot by action or emotion; to make feverish.
To excite ardour in; to rouse to action; to excite to excess; to inflame, as the passions.
To arouse, to excite (sexually).
definitions.example: The massage heated her up.

definitions.definition: A system that raises the temperature of a room or building. Compare heater.
The act of making something hot.
definitions.example: The crucible was subjected to several heatings.

definitions.definition: Causing heat.
One who installs.
A program that installs software and prepares it for use.
A figure carved in relief or incised, especially representing a sound, word, or idea.
Any non-verbal symbol that imparts information.
A visual representation of a letter, character, or symbol, in a specific font and style.
A vertical groove.
A laptop computer.
The ability to be enforced, or to be enforced to any useful effect.
definitions.example: The international community's sanctions against the regime had some teeth to them this time around.

definitions.definition: To grow teeth.
definitions.example: Babies typically start teething at about six months.

definitions.definition: To bite on something to relieve discomfort caused by growing teeth.
definitions.example: She'll teethe on anything that she can get into her mouth.

definitions.definition: A hard, calcareous structure present in the mouth of many vertebrate animals, generally used for eating.
A sharp projection on the blade of a saw or similar implement.
A projection on the edge of a gear that meshes with similar projections on adjacent gears, or on the circumference of a cog that engages with a chain.
A projection or point in other parts of the body resembling the tooth of a vertebrate animal.
A pointed projection from the margin of a leaf.
The rough surface of some kinds of cel or other films that allows better adhesion of artwork.
Liking, fondness (compare toothsome).
definitions.example: I have a sweet tooth: I love sugary treats.

definitions.definition: An irreducible component of a comb that intersects the handle in exactly one point, that point being distinct from the unique point of intersection for any other tooth of the comb.
(usually used predicatively, not attributively, be afraid) Impressed with fear or apprehension; in fear.
definitions.example: He is afraid of death.

definitions.definition: Regretful, sorry.
definitions.example: I am afraid I cannot help you in this matter.

definitions.definition: (used with for) Worried about, feeling concern for, fearing for (someone or something).
To connect an object, especially a ship or a boat, to a fixed point.
To cast anchor; to come to anchor.
definitions.example: Our ship (or the captain) anchored in the stream.

definitions.definition: To stop; to fix or rest.
To provide emotional stability for a person in distress.
To perform as an anchorman or anchorwoman.
To be stuck; to be unable to move away from a position.
Having the extremities turned back, like the flukes of an anchor.
definitions.example: an anchored cross

definitions.definition: An abnormal or irrational fear of arachnids (especially spiders).
The act of achieving or performing; a successful performance; accomplishment
A great or heroic deed or feat; something accomplished by valor or boldness
An escutcheon or ensign armorial; now generally applied to the funeral shield commonly called hatchment.
An award for completing a particular task or meeting an objective in a video game.
definitions.example: Finishing the game does not give you a 100% score until you have unlocked all of the achievements.

definitions.definition: (grammar) The lexical aspect (aktionsart) of verbs or predicates that change in an instant.
Not closed
Not physically drawn together, closed, folded or contracted; extended
definitions.example: an open hand; an open flower

definitions.definition: Actively conducting or prepared to conduct business.
definitions.example: Banks are not open on bank holidays.

definitions.definition: Receptive.
definitions.example: I am open to new ideas.

definitions.definition: Public
definitions.example: He published an open letter to the governor on a full page of the New York Times.

definitions.definition: Candid, ingenuous, not subtle in character.
definitions.example: The man is an open book.

definitions.definition: (of a formula) Having a free variable.
(of a set) Which is part of a predefined collection of subsets of X, that defines a topological space on X.
(of a walk) Whose first and last vertices are different.
(of a file, document, etc.) In current use; mapped to part of memory.
definitions.example: I couldn't save my changes because another user had the same file open.

definitions.definition: Not fulfilled.
definitions.example: I've got open orders for as many containers of red durum as you can get me.

definitions.definition: Not settled or adjusted; not decided or determined; not closed or withdrawn from consideration.
definitions.example: an open question

definitions.definition: (stringed instruments) Of a note, played without pressing the string against the fingerboard.
(wind instruments) Of a note, played without closing any finger-hole, key or valve.
Not of a quality to prevent communication, as by closing waterways, blocking roads, etc.; hence, not frosty or inclement; mild; used of the weather or the climate.
definitions.example: an open winter

definitions.definition: (Of correspondence) Written or sent with the intention that it may made public or referred to at any trial, rather than by way of confidential private negotiation for a settlement. (Opposite of "without prejudice")
definitions.example: You will observe that this is an open letter and we reserve the right to mention it to the judge should the matter come to trial.

definitions.definition: Uttered with a relatively wide opening of the articulating organs; said of vowels.
Uttered, as a consonant, with the oral passage simply narrowed without closure.
(of a syllable) That ends in a vowel; not having a coda.
Made public, usable with a free licence and without proprietary components.
Resulting from an incision, puncture or any other process by which the skin no longer protects an internal part of the body.
To quickly lower the head or body in order to prevent it from being struck by something.
To quickly lower (the head) in order to prevent it from being struck by something.
To lower (something) into water; to thrust or plunge under liquid and suddenly withdraw.
To go under the surface of water and immediately reappear; to plunge one's head into water or other liquid.
To bow.
To evade doing something.
To lower the volume of (a sound) so that other sounds in the mix can be heard more clearly.
To enter a place for a short moment.
definitions.example: I'm just going to duck into the loo for a minute, can you hold my bag?

definitions.definition: The action of the verb to duck.
An instance of ducking (a person in water, etc).
definitions.example: They gave him a ducking in the river as a punishment.

definitions.definition: A substance that acts to increase physiological or nervous activity in the body.
Something that promotes activity, interest, or enthusiasm.
(pharmaceutical effect) Acting as a stimulant.
To censure (someone or something); to criticize.
To bring into disrepute.

(usually followed by "for") To assert or consider that someone is the cause of something negative; to place blame, to attribute responsibility (for something negative or for doing something negative).
definitions.example: The arsonist was blamed for the fire.

definitions.definition: God-forsaken.
Variant of profane damn.
definitions.example: His damned cards are scattered!

definitions.definition: To support (someone), to back up or assist (someone) in an action.
To keep up; to preserve; to uphold (a state, condition etc.).
To declare or affirm (a clause) to be true; to assert.
In the manner of a monument.
Large, grand and imposing.
Taking a great amount of time and effort to complete.
Relating to monuments.
definitions.example: Monumental construction.

definitions.definition: Short for mainline.
To mainly play a specific character, or side, during a game.
definitions.example: He mains the same character as me in that game.

definitions.definition: Of a road: to convert into a main or primary road.
Of chief or leading importance; prime, principal.
Chief, most important, or principal in extent, size, or strength; consisting of the largest part.
definitions.example: main timbers  main branch of a river  main body of an army

definitions.definition: (of force, strength, etc.) Full, sheer, undivided.
Big; angry.
Belonging to or connected with the principal mast in a vessel.
Great in size or degree; important, powerful, strong, vast.
Exceedingly, extremely, greatly, mightily, very, very much.
Deceptive or false appearance; that which misleads the eye or the mind.
An argument, or apparent argument, which professes to be decisive of the matter at issue, while in reality it is not. A specious argument.
To flutter; palpitate.
To hang loosely; flag.
To beat by flapping.
Being lower; being beneath something.
Under anesthesia, especially general anesthesia; sedated.
definitions.example: Ensure the patient is sufficiently under.

definitions.definition: In a way lower or less than.
In a way inferior to.
In an unconscious state.
definitions.example: It took the hypnotist several minutes to make his subject go under.

definitions.definition: In or at a lower level than.
As a subject of; subordinate to.
definitions.example: He served in World War II under General Omar Bradley.

definitions.definition: Less than.
Below the surface of.
In the face of; in response to (some attacking force).
definitions.example: to collapse under stress; to give in under interrogation

definitions.definition: As, in the character of.
definitions.example: he writes books under the name John Smith

definitions.definition: A system for transferring messages from one computer to another, usually through a network.
definitions.example: He sent me his details via email.

definitions.definition: A quantity of messages sent through an email system.
definitions.example: I am searching through my old email.

definitions.definition: A message sent through an email system.
definitions.example: He sent me an email last week to remind me about the meeting.

definitions.definition: An email address.
definitions.example: Don’t send personal messages to my work email.

definitions.definition: To send an email or emails to.
definitions.example: She emailed me last week, asking about the status of the project.

definitions.definition: (may take two objects) To send (something) through email.
definitions.example: He emailed the file out to everyone.

definitions.definition: To send, or compose and send, an email or emails.
definitions.example: Most teenagers seem to spend almost the whole day emailing and surfing the Web.

definitions.definition: Enamel.

A written instrument, such as a deed, temporarily deposited with a neutral third party (the escrow agent), by the agreement of two parties to a valid contract. The escrow agent will deliver the document to the benefited party when the conditions of the contract have been met. The depositor has no control over the instrument in escrow.
In common law, escrow applied to the deposits only of instruments for conveyance of land, but it now applies to all instruments so deposited.
Money or other property so deposited is also loosely referred to as escrow.
The state of property deposited with an escrow agent.
To place in escrow.
Relating to the sky or outer space, regarded as the realm of the sun, moon, planets, and stars.
Of or pertaining to the highest degree of glory.
Of or pertaining to the heaven believed in by many religions.
Of or pertaining to the kingdom of God; divine.
Strongly or sublimely beautiful or pleasurable.
In a manner like that of heaven; by the influence or agency of heaven; divinely, miraculously.
To a degree such as to elicit great pleasure.
Mischievous; of or befitting an imp.
A vile, wicked person.
In fiction, a character who has the role of being bad, especially antagonizing the hero.
Any opponent player, especially a hypothetical player for example and didactic purposes. Compare: hero.
definitions.example: Let's discuss how to play if you are the chip leader (that is, if you have more chips than all the villains).

definitions.definition: To debase; to degrade.
A feudal tenant.
A money prize pool which accumulates until the conditions are met for it to be won.
A large cash prize or money.
An unexpected windfall or reward.
A concentration of surface wood or fuel.
definitions.example: a juniper jackpot

definitions.definition: A large accumulated point bonus, originally awarded after a long set of actions but now often easily available in multiball modes.
(of a slot machine) To issue a jackpot.
To touch repeatedly or stroke delicately in a manner which causes laughter, pleasure and twitching.
definitions.example: He tickled Nancy's tummy, and she started to giggle.

definitions.definition: To unexpectedly touch or stroke delicately in a manner which causes displeasure or withdrawal.
definitions.example: A stranger tickled Nancy's tummy, causing her to scream in fear.

definitions.definition: (of a body part) To feel as if the body part in question is being tickled.
definitions.example: My nose tickles, and I'm going to sneeze!

definitions.definition: To appeal to someone's taste, curiosity etc.
To cause delight or amusement in.
definitions.example: He was tickled to receive such a wonderful gift.

definitions.definition: To feel titillation.
To catch fish in the hand (usually in rivers or smaller streams) by manually stimulating the fins.
To be excited or heartened.
A hypothetical particle that travels faster than the speed of light.
A flaw in a physical theory that predicts the existence of tachyons or similar paradoxical results. Compare with ghost.
The territory of a town.
A subdivision of a county.
(Pre 1994) An area set aside for nonwhite occupation.
(Post 1994) A nonwhite (usually subeconomic) area attached to a city.
A small town.
A go-cart.
To ride in a go-cart.
To make cold, to sharpen.
Often with a prepositional phrase, or with to and an infinitive: showing a quick and ardent responsiveness or willingness; eager, enthusiastic, interested.
definitions.example: I’m keen on computers.

definitions.definition: Fierce, intense, vehement.
definitions.example: This boy has a keen appetite.

definitions.definition: Having a fine edge or point; sharp.
Acute of mind, having or expressing mental acuteness; penetrating, sharp.
Acrimonious, bitter, piercing.
definitions.example: keen satire or sarcasm

definitions.definition: Of cold, wind, etc.: cutting, penetrating, piercing, sharp.
definitions.example: a keen wind

definitions.definition: Of prices, extremely low as to be competitive.
Marvelous.
definitions.example: I just got this peachy keen new dress.

definitions.definition: Brave, courageous; audacious, bold.
A short, stout person or animal, especially one of a breed of very short-legged chickens.
A small bottle of beer.
Short and thick; stout or stocky
The act of destroying.
definitions.example: The destruction of the condemned building will take place at noon.

definitions.definition: The results of a destructive event.
definitions.example: Amid the seemingly endless destruction, a single flower bloomed.

definitions.definition: A unit of thirty to forty soldiers typically commanded by a lieutenant and forming part of a company.
A group of self-driving vehicles travelling in a close convoy and communicating electronically with each other.
To alternate starts with a teammate of opposite handedness, depending on the handedness of the opposing pitcher
definitions.example: Taylor has been hitting poorly against left-handers, and Morgan has been hitting poorly against right-handers, so they will platoon.

definitions.definition: Of self-driving vehicles: to travel in a close convoy, each vehicle communicating electronically with the others.
A flat pastry pressed with a grid pattern.
definitions.example: The brunch was waffles with strawberries and whipped cream.

definitions.definition: A potato waffle, a savoury flat potato cake with the same kind of grid pattern.
To smash.
The variation of strong and weak elements (such as duration, accent) of sounds, notably in speech or music, over time; a beat or meter.
definitions.example: Dance to the rhythm of the music.

definitions.definition: A specifically defined pattern of such variation.
definitions.example: Most dances have a rhythm as distinctive as the Iambic verse in poetry

definitions.definition: A flow, repetition or regularity.
definitions.example: Once you get the rhythm of it, the job will become easy.

definitions.definition: The tempo or speed of a beat, song or repetitive event.
definitions.example: We walked with a quick, even rhythm.

definitions.definition: The musical instruments which provide rhythm (mainly; not or less melody) in a musical ensemble.
definitions.example: The Baroque term basso continuo is virtually equivalent to rhythm

definitions.definition: A regular quantitative change in a variable (notably natural) process.
definitions.example: The rhythm of the seasons dominates agriculture as well as wildlife

definitions.definition: Controlled repetition of a phrase, incident or other element as a stylistic figure in literature and other narrative arts; the effect it creates.
definitions.example: The running gag is a popular rhythm in motion pictures and theater comedy

definitions.definition: A member of the class of animals Aves in the phylum Chordata, characterized by being warm-blooded, having feathers and wings usually capable of flight, and laying eggs.
definitions.example: Ducks and sparrows are birds.

definitions.definition: A man, fellow.
A girl or woman, especially one considered sexually attractive.
Girlfriend.
definitions.example: Mike went out with his bird last night.

definitions.definition: An airplane.
A satellite.
A chicken; the young of a fowl; a young eaglet; a nestling.
A prison sentence.
definitions.example: He’s doing bird.

definitions.definition: A yardbird
To utter words indistinctly or unintelligibly; to utter inarticulate sounds
definitions.example: The men were babbling, so we couldn't make sense of anything.

definitions.definition: To talk incoherently; to utter meaningless words.
To talk too much; to chatter; to prattle.
To make a continuous murmuring noise, like shallow water running over stones.
definitions.example: Hounds are said to babble, or to be babbling, when they are too noisy after having found a good scent.

definitions.definition: To utter in an indistinct or incoherent way; to repeat words or sounds in a childish way without understanding.
To reveal; to give away (a secret).
A human; someone susceptible to death.
definitions.example: Her wisdom was beyond that of a mere mortal.

definitions.definition: Susceptible to death by aging, sickness, injury, or wound; not immortal.
Causing death; deadly, fatal, killing, lethal (now only of wounds, injuries etc.).
Punishable by death.
Fatally vulnerable.
Of or relating to the time of death.
Affecting as if with power to kill; deathly.
definitions.example: mortal enemy

definitions.definition: Human; belonging or pertaining to people who are mortal.
definitions.example: mortal wit or knowledge; mortal power

definitions.definition: Very painful or tedious; wearisome.
definitions.example: a sermon lasting two mortal hours

definitions.definition: Very drunk; wasted; smashed.
Of a sin: involving the penalty of spiritual death, rather than merely venial.
Mortally; enough to cause death.
definitions.example: It's mortal cold out there.

definitions.definition: To divide into two or more branches.
definitions.example: A road, a tree, or a stream forks.

definitions.definition: To move with a fork (as hay or food).
To spawn a new child process in some sense duplicating the existing process.
To split a (software) project into several projects.
To split a (software) distributed version control repository
To kick someone in the crotch.
To shoot into blades, as corn does.
To bale a shaft dry.
That splits into two or more directions, or parts.
definitions.example: It's a forked road. Turn left at the fork.

definitions.example: 1945: I got into the bus full of taxpayers who were giving some money to a taxpayer who had on his taxpayer's stomach a little box which allowed the other taxpayers to continue their taxpayers' journeys. I noticed in this bus a taxpayer with a long taxpayer's neck and whose taxpayer's head bore a taxpayer's felt hat encircled by a plait the like of which no taxpayer ever wore before. Suddenly the said taxpayer peremptorily addressed a nearby taxpayer, complaining bitterly that he was purposely treading on his taxpayer's toes every time other taxpayers got on or off the taxpayers' bus. Then the angry taxpayer went and sat down in a seat for taxpayers which another taxpayer had just vacated. — Raymond Queneau, 'from Polyptotes' in Exercises in Style, 1945 (Eng. 1958)

definitions.definition: Spatially first, placed at the beginning, in the first position; especially said of the first letter of a word.
definitions.example: The initial letter of names is usually printed with a capital letter.

definitions.definition: To move heavily and clumsily, or with a sense of prancing and triumph.
A hard dark sandstone with poorly sorted angular grains of quartz, feldspar, and small rock fragments in a compact, clay-fine matrix.
Twelve dozen = 144.
The total nominal earnings or amount, before taxes, expenses, exceptions or similar are deducted. That which remains after all deductions is called net.
The bulk, the mass, the masses.
To earn money, not including expenses.
definitions.example: The movie grossed three million on the first weekend.

definitions.definition: (of behaviour considered to be wrong) Highly or conspicuously offensive.
definitions.example: a gross mistake;  gross injustice;  gross negligence; a gross insult

definitions.definition: (of an amount) Excluding any deductions; including all associated amounts.
definitions.example: gross domestic product; gross income; gross weight

definitions.definition: Seen without a microscope (usually for a tissue or an organ); at a large scale; not detailed.
definitions.example: gross anatomy

definitions.definition: Causing disgust.
definitions.example: I threw up all over the bed. It was totally gross.

definitions.definition: Lacking refinement in behaviour or manner; offending a standard of morality.
(of a product) Lacking refinement; not of high quality.
(of a person) Heavy in proportion to one's height; having a lot of excess flesh.
Not sensitive in perception or feeling.
Difficult or impossible to see through.
Easy to perceive.
A broadcast of the news; a news report that is transmitted over the air for television, radio, etc.
Under normal conditions or circumstances; usually; most of the time
definitions.example: Normally, I eat breakfast at 6am, but today, I got up late and didn't eat until 9.

definitions.definition: In the expected or customary manner.
To a usual or customary extent or degree.
definitions.example: He was abnormally agitated, she only normally so.

definitions.definition: In the manner of a variable with a Gaussian distribution.
